# High Performance Python, Third Edition (Micha Gorelick & Ian Ozsvald)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Understanding Performant Python](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/01-understanding-performant-python.md): Writing, optimizing, refactoring, profiling, or architecting Python code, particularly in the domains of scientific computing, data processing, machine learning, or general performance-critical applications, including structuring Python projects, converting Jupyter Notebooks to production code, and implementing testing and deployment pipelines.
    - [Profiling to Find Bottlenecks](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/02-profiling-to-find-bottlenecks.md): Performance optimization, execution speed measurement, memory footprint analysis, bottleneck identification, and profiling of Python code, including efficiency improvements and resource usage reduction.
    - [Lists and Tuples](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/03-lists-and-tuples.md): Python programming tasks involving data structure selection, performance optimization of collections, memory footprint reduction, memory allocation optimization, and search/lookup algorithm implementation.
    - [Dictionaries and Sets](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/04-dictionaries-and-sets.md): Optimizing data lookups, deduplicating data, managing unordered collections, implementing custom classes intended for use as keys or unique items, or analyzing the performance and memory footprint of Python dictionaries (`dict`) and sets (`set`).
    - [Iterators and Generators](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/05-iterators-and-generators.md): Python development tasks involving data pipelines, sequence processing, loops, large dataset handling, memory optimization, and mathematical or infinite series generation. Trigger these rules when refactoring list comprehensions, writing data ingest functions, mitigating `MemoryError` issues, or optimizing CPU/Memory tradeoffs in loops.
    - [Matrix and Vector Computation](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/06-matrix-and-vector-computation.md): Performance optimization, numerical computation, matrix/vector operations, CPU/GPU profiling, deep learning pipeline optimization, or when working with numpy, numexpr, or torch (PyTorch) in Python.
    - [Pandas, Dask, and Polars](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/07-pandas-dask-and-polars.md): Tabular data processing, dataframe manipulation, time-series data analysis, or performance optimization for data science pipelines using Python.
    - [Compiling to C](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/08-compiling-to-c.md): Optimizing CPU-bound mathematical operations, loops with heavy intermediate object creation, compiling Python to machine code, applying JIT/AOT compilers, parallelizing algorithms using OpenMP, or interfacing with native C/Fortran/Rust libraries (FFI).
    - [Asynchronous I/O](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/09-asynchronous-i-o.md): When the AI is asked to write, refactor, optimize, or debug Python code involving I/O operations (such as network requests, database queries, API clients, web scrapers, or file reads/writes), or workloads that mix heavy CPU computation with intermittent I/O tasks.
    - [The multiprocessing Module](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/10-the-multiprocessing-module.md): Parallelizing Python code, optimizing CPU-bound tasks, utilizing the multiprocessing or joblib modules, implementing Interprocess Communication (IPC), managing concurrent workers/pools, or sharing large data structures (like NumPy arrays) across multiple Python processes.
    - [Clusters and Job Queues](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/11-clusters-and-job-queues.md): Tasks involving the design, implementation, debugging, or deployment of distributed computing solutions, clustered Python applications, task queues, message brokers (Pub/Sub or Queues), IPython Parallel setups, or Docker containerization for horizontal scaling.
    - [Using Less RAM](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/12-using-less-ram.md): Optimizing Python code for memory usage, reducing RAM footprints, handling large datasets, performing memory profiling, selecting data structures for high-volume text or numerics, managing large sparse matrices, or implementing approximate/probabilistic counting and membership algorithms.
    - [Lessons from the Field](.roo/vendor/python/high-performance-python-third-edition-micha-gorelick-ian-ozsvald/13-lessons-from-the-field.md): High performance Python programming, data science pipelines, machine learning engineering, large-scale web scraping, and quantitative finance system development.
